steps.title: Spot the goal-directed behavior
steps.content: The passage emphasizes their attempt to make stones “as round as possible” — not random chipping, but purposeful work toward a specific outcome. This goal-directed behavior is what would signal cognitive sophistication.

steps.title: Apply the meaning to the choices
steps.content: The blank requires a word meaning “done with purpose and intention, working toward a specific goal.” Deliberate expresses this exactly — it captures the methodical, intentional effort to achieve the specific result of roundness. Concerning relates to worry, comparable requires a missing comparison, and sympathetic means emotionally compassionate—none fit describing goal-directed work aimed at a specific outcome. That matches choice D — D is correct.
